question: Which line correctly prints the text Hello, world! in Java?
feedback: System.out.println is the standard way to print a line of text. Note the capital S in System, the dot-separated calls, the double quotes around the string, and the trailing semicolon — all four are required.

question: Which of the following are valid Java variable declarations?
feedback: Java declarations are type-then-name-then-value. Variable names cannot start with a digit, single quotes are reserved for char literals, and = (not ==) is the assignment operator.

question: Which keyword is used to declare a class in Java?
feedback: Java is case-sensitive — the keyword is the lowercase class. Class (capital C) refers instead to the java.lang.Class type used for reflection.

question: The method that serves as the entry point for every Java program is called ___.
feedback: Every standalone Java program starts at public static void main(String[] args). The JVM looks specifically for a method named main with that exact signature.

question: The single-character symbol that ends almost every statement in Java is ___.
feedback: The semicolon terminates simple statements in Java. Forgetting it is one of the most common compile errors for beginners.

question: Match each Java type to the kind of value it stores.

pairs.definition: A whole number such as 42 or -7
A sequence of characters in double quotes

feedback: int and double are primitive numeric types; boolean is the primitive truth-value type; String is a reference type from java.lang for text. These four cover most beginner code.

question: Match each Java operator to what it does.

pairs.definition: Tests whether two values are equal
Assigns a value to a variable
Tests whether two values are not equal
Returns the remainder after integer division

feedback: Confusing = (assignment) with == (equality test) is one of the classic Java bugs. The remainder operator % is especially handy for checking even/odd: n % 2 == 0.

question: What does this program print?
feedback: The loop runs with i = 1, 2, 3. Each iteration concatenates the string "Hi " with the current value of i and prints it on its own line.

question: What does this if/else print?
feedback: 7 % 2 is 1, not 0, so the condition is false and the else branch runs — printing odd.

question: What does this while loop print?
feedback: n doubles each pass: 1 → 2 → 4 → 8 → 16 → 32. After the assignment to 32, the condition (32 < 20) is false and the loop exits, so 32 is printed.
